channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Election 2024: Elon Musk's Reckless Rhetoric and the Threat to Democracy
item.description: Elon Musk's recent speech at a Trump rally has sparked concerns about his reckless approach to politics, as he claims that a Harris administration would be the last election in America.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Google ordered to open up Play app store to competition
item.description: A U.S. judge on Monday ordered Alphabet’s Google to overhaul its mobile app business to give Android users more options to download apps and to pay for transactions within them, following a jury verdict last year for Fortnite maker Epic Games. The injunction by U.S.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: This tiny super cheap $13 PC is barely bigger than an adult pinkie finger and has no OS — and yet this hacker-friendly device packs a display, MicroSD card slot plus Wi-Fi connectivity
item.description: Waveshare’s ESP32-S3-LCD-1.47 compact USB dongle packs a display, MicroSD card slot, and Wi-Fi connectivity
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: American Water, largest water utility in US, dealing with cyberattack
item.description: The largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States announced Monday that it was the victim of a cyberattack, prompting the firm to pause billing to customers.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge orders Google to open its Android app store to competition
item.description: A federal judge has ordered Google to tear down the digital walls shielding its Android app store from competition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Titan's former lead engineer says he felt pressured to get the submersible ready
item.description: The lead engineer for an experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreck of the Titanic testified Monday that he felt pressured to get the vessel ready to dive and refused to pilot it for a journey several years earlier.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and LISA J. ADAMS WAGNER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Unicorns wanted: Navigating the Product Owner shortage in IT
item.description: This article was co-authored by Kira Kessel, Associate at Metis Strategy. You’ve seen the virtues of transforming from a project to a product operating model: value-driven work, delivered by dedicated teams rather than through projects led by disparate team members.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Hurricane Helene: Combatting False Rumors Amid Disaster Relief Efforts
item.description: Government officials and experts combat false rumors about disaster funds and weather control amidst rescue efforts following Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Judge rules the FTC can proceed with antitrust lawsuit against Amazon, tosses out few state claims
item.description: A federal judge said the Federal Trade Commission can proceed with its landmark antitrust lawsuit against Amazon.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: EU ruling clamps down on Meta’s use of personal data for ads
item.description: The European Court of Justice has decided that Facebook owner Meta must minimize the amount of personal data it uses for personalized ads, the BBC reports . The decision from the EU’s top court means that only a small part of Meta’s data collection can be used for advertisements.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: This new AI answer engine plans to pay media companies for their content
item.description: When ChatGPT launched two years ago, it blew open the doors to a new way to search for information online. Instead of sifting through a search engine’s list of links, users can now query a chatbot and get an actual answer.
item.dc:creator: David Salazar, Fast Company

item.title: A new tool to help startups get a foot in the door at the Pentagon
item.description: "There are only 75 PEO offices inside the four services. And I decided to go figure out who they were," said Steve Blank. The post A new tool to help startups get a foot in the door at the Pentagon first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Digital skills needed for North America’s economic future
item.description: At the Exceptional Women Alliance (EWA), we enable high-level women to mentor each other to achieve personal and professional happiness through sisterhood.
item.dc:creator: Larraine Segil, Fast Company

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg passes Jeff Bezos to become world’s second-richest person
item.description: Zuckerberg’s bet on the metaverse — which initially looked like a huge bust — has paid off in recent months, pushing his net worth to a high-water mark of $206.2 billion.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Los intangibles del CIO: seis habilidades que diferencian a los ejecutivos de TI eficaces
item.description: “Lo que le ha traído hasta aquí no le llevará hasta allí”. Este consejo del coach ejecutivo y autor de bestsellers Marshall Goldsmith nunca ha sido tan cierto para los CIO como hoy.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: French government leaves $1.1 billion hole in Atos refinancing plan, Atos says don’t worry
item.description: Atos has failed to agree terms with the French government for the sale of part of its business, one of several elements of its plan to refinance its debt.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: These are the 7 most devastating data breaches. Now what?
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson: Data breaches in 2024 exposed info of millions, stressing the need for better cybersecurity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bay Area poll: Silicon Valley is too powerful, has lost its moral compass, majority say
item.description: Interviews with poll respondents reveal nuanced views and deep disenchantment with Silicon Valley tech.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: CIO intangibles: 6 abilities that set effective IT execs apart
item.description: What got you here won’t get you there. That advice from executive coach and bestselling author Marshall Goldsmith has never been truer for CIOs than it is today.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Tech Day of Pink: Estée Lauder CIO’s global IT movement to fund breast cancer research
item.description: When he joined The Estée Lauder Companies in 2017, Michael Smith recognized a way he could help make a difference in a different way than his typical CIO duties: raise awareness about breast cancer.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 5 ways companies can use time series forecasting
item.description: Time series forecasting is a powerful machine learning method that leverages historical time-stamped data to predict future events and help reduce uncertainty from business conditions — for example, to accurately predict sales, inventory levels, and even manufacturing data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nobel Prize in medicine honors 2 scientists for their discovery of microRNA
item.description: Two scientists have won the Nobel Prize in physiology or medicine for their discovery of microRNA, tiny bits of genetic material that serve as on and off switches inside cells that help control what the cells do and when they do it.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Niemann And Mike Corder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: ChatGPT o1-preview excels at code generation
item.description: “Ho, hum,” I thought in response to the release of the OpenAI o1 models. “Yet another stochastic parrot.” I was wrong. In fact, I tried the o1-preview model on one of my standard programming tasks, and found it to be surprisingly good.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Advocacy groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending Swiss criminal probe of woman's death
item.description: Advocacy groups behind a so-called suicide capsule said Sunday they have suspended the process of taking applications to use it — which numbered over 370 last month — as a criminal investigation into its first use in Switzerland is completed.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A flying shopping cart could make your future grocery runs that much easier
item.description: A flying shopping cart could revolutionize the way we shop and transport things. The Palletrone is a hovering platform that users push around at chest height.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk makes his first appearance at a Trump rally and casts the election in dire terms
item.description: The appearance marked the first time Musk joined one of Trump’s trademark rallies and represented the growing alliance between the two men in the final stretch of a competitive presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: How to remotely fix family computer woes
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explores how you can help troubleshoot a computer issue remotely on Windows or a Mac. There are a variety of options.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk will attend Trump’s Pennsylvania rally at site of first assassination attempt
item.description: Elon Musk is set to attend Donald Trump’s campaign rally in Butler, Pennsylvania, on Saturday, marking an even deeper show of the billionaire’s support for the former president and his shift to the right.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: The shocking rise and fall of elite cybersecurity firm IronNet
item.description: The future was once dazzling for IronNet. Founded by a former director of the National Security Agency and stacked with elite members of the U.S. intelligence establishment, IronNet promised it was going to revolutionize the way governments and corporations combat cyberattacks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Fiscal Service using death data to reduce improper payments
item.description: Tim Gribben, the commissioner of the Bureau of the Fiscal Service, said the bureau refined and reframed its progress statement for the first time since 2018. The post Fiscal Service using death data to reduce improper payments first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Civilization 7 makers work with Shawnee to bring sincere representation of the tribe to the game
item.description: MIAMI, Okla. (AP) — Shawnee Tribe Chief Ben Barnes grew up playing video games, including “probably hundreds of hours” colonizing a distant planet in the 1999 title Sid Meier's Alpha Centauri.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Understanding exit codes on Linux
item.description: Whenever you run a command in a Linux terminal, a numeric exit code is generated – even if you see no sign of it. The code reports the success or failure of the command. And, if the command fails, it provides a value that hints at the problem. If the exit code is 0, all is well.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What’s new for Apple Intelligence?
item.description: Most Apple watchers may have noticed that the company’s iPhone 16 marketing really does put Apple Intelligence front and center, even though its home-baked breed of AaI (Artificial [Apple] Intelligence) isn’t available quite yet.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
